title: Incident runbook
purpose: Response playbook for outage, migration, and rollback incidents during the Firebase-to-platform transition.

sev1.definition: Critical production outage or data integrity risk affecting core request flows.

sev2.definition: Major degradation with workarounds; no confirmed data loss.

sev3.definition: Minor degradation or non-critical function impact.

roles.incidentCommander: Coordinates triage, ownership, and decision flow.
roles.operationsLead: Executes mitigations, rollback toggles, and system checks.
roles.applicationLead: Owns app/service diagnosis and code-level remediation.
roles.communicationsLead: Publishes stakeholder/customer updates and status timeline.
roles.scribe: Captures timeline, decisions, and action items.

globalResponseFlow.name: Detect and classify
actions: Validate alert signal against metrics/logs baseline.
Open incident channel and timeline record.

globalResponseFlow.name: Stabilize
actions: Apply immediate safeguards (traffic shaping, feature flag disable, queue pause).
Preserve forensic data (logs, traces, failing payload samples).

globalResponseFlow.name: Diagnose and mitigate
actions: Localize fault domain: API, client integration, data pipeline, or legacy dependency.
Track mitigation checkpoints every 10 minutes for SEV1.

globalResponseFlow.name: Recover and validate
actions: Run smoke checks for login/request/note/media critical paths.
Confirm metrics return to baseline thresholds.
Document customer impact window and residual risk.

globalResponseFlow.name: Close and follow-up
actions: Create remediation tickets with owners and due dates.
Schedule post-incident review within 2 business days for SEV1/SEV2.

playbooks.name: Platform/API outage
triggers: p95 latency sustained above alert threshold
triageChecks: Service health endpoints and dependency reachability
Database connection pool and queue backlogs
immediateMitigation: Route eligible traffic to fallback legacy paths using cutover flags.
Scale service replicas/workers if resource saturation detected.
Disable non-critical background workloads to preserve core request throughput.
rollbackDecisionCriteria: SEV1 persists >15 minutes despite mitigation attempts
Core request flows still failing above error budget
exitCriteria: Error and latency metrics within baseline

playbooks.name: Migration discrepancy incident
triggers: Data mismatch reports between legacy and platform records
triageChecks: Validate latest migration batch IDs and replay logs
immediateMitigation: Pause migration and downstream sync jobs.
Lock affected writes if integrity risk is active.
Use last-known-good snapshot and replay candidate dry run.
containmentRules: No irreversible data mutation until discrepancy root cause identified.
All replay operations require dual approval (operations + application lead).
exitCriteria: Parity checks pass on remediated scope

playbooks.name: Cutover regression with rollback
triggers: User-facing failures immediately after enabling API cutover switch
Notification/history behavior divergence beyond acceptable threshold
triageChecks: Identify impacted surface (web-admin, portal, android, ios)
Correlate failure to specific feature flag/state transition
Compare control cohort versus canary behavior
rollbackActions: Disable impacted surface cutover flag(s) only.
Confirm traffic and behavior revert to legacy path.
Run focused smoke test for recovered path.
postRollbackActions: Open defect ticket with rollback timestamp and blast radius.
Re-enter rollout at previous successful stage after fix.
exitCriteria: Service stabilized and customer journey recovered

verificationChecklist: Alert storm resolved; no new high-severity alerts for 30 minutes
Data integrity/parity checks pass for affected scope
Rollback/cutover flags left in intended post-incident state
